note: Manual layer of dashboard/portfolio-hq.html: everything here comes from live PostHog reads, founder statements, or weekly readouts — not derivable from status.json. Update this file (and bump asOf), then run ./agentic-os refresh — it regenerates portfolio-hq.html as its last step.

founderCommand.title: Ship the Resumely export-observability fix (tests pass, nothing deployed)
founderCommand.body: codex/fix-web-export-observability (ResumeBuilder web repo) adds the event instrumentation that was missing on the whole export path — this is the fix for the biggest blind spot in the funnel, not a cosmetic change. Contract test went 3/3 fail → 3/3 pass, targeted + full lint clean, production build succeeds, TypeScript clean outside pre-existing test debt. 5 files are uncommitted, 0 commits ahead of origin/main, nothing pushed or deployed. Every day this sits uncommitted is a day of real web traffic still generating zero export telemetry.
founderCommand.prompt: In the ResumeBuilder web repo (new-ResumeBuilder-ai-, worktree export-observability, branch codex/fix-web-export-observability): review the 5 uncommitted files, commit, push, open a PR, and deploy. After deploy, watch PostHog by person_id filtered to platform=web, excluding founder prefixes, is_internal_tester=true, founder email, and the +qa email convention — re-read after the first 5 real web completers of optimized_viewed → export_cta_seen → export_pdf_tapped → export_started → export_success/export_failed.

founderCommand.title: Commit and push RunSmart WP-40 S1 before it goes stale
founderCommand.body: The Apple Health onboarding step (OnboardingView.swift) is implemented and simulator-validated — TDD red→green, debug build passed, Health prompt appears, Skip advances to Ready, Connect follows the real HealthKit path. The branch has no upstream and is uncommitted, including pre-existing partial S2 service changes. This is exactly the stranded-work pattern flagged repeatedly this week (three prior incidents, PR #89/#90/#116) — push it now even if S2-S4 and physical-device QA aren't done yet.
founderCommand.prompt: In the RunSmart iOS repo (IOS RunSmart light): commit the WP-40 S1 changes (OnboardingView.swift, RunSmartReadinessTests.swift, wp40-simulator-qa.md, task progress/session memory), push to a named branch with upstream set, and open a draft PR so the work survives between sessions. Do not merge — S2-S4 and physical-device QA are still open.

tracks.name: RunSmart WP-40 / Apple Health activation
tracks.standing: S1 is implemented and validated in the simulator (2026-07-10): the skippable Apple Health onboarding step is live in OnboardingView.swift, reusing the existing HealthKit connection route and analytics. S2-S4 and physical-device QA were intentionally not started this session. The branch (including pre-existing partial S2 service changes) has no upstream and is fully uncommitted — same stranded-work risk already named in this week's OS-hygiene priority.

items.text: Skippable Apple Health onboarding step added (OnboardingView.swift), reusing the existing HealthKit connection route and analytics; Connect/Skip accessibility identifiers wired

TDD red state reproduced then final focused test passed (1/1); debug simulator build passed; Health prompt appeared during onboarding; Skip advanced to Ready; Connect followed the real HealthKit path; git diff --check passed; only known warning is a pre-existing deprecated HKWorkout initializer
S2-S4 and physical-device QA not started. Branch has no upstream and remains uncommitted — commit and push before continuing, nothing has been lost yet but nothing is protected either

tracks.name: Resumely web export — observability fix
tracks.standing: A fresh non-founder QA run completed the full production funnel (signup → upload → review → approve → real browser PDF download, no console errors) on 2026-07-10, proving the export mechanism itself works today. Separately, 7 real organic web users reached optimization pages and generated zero export telemetry — but that cannot be read as 'the export button is broken': the old web button was a raw redirect with zero tracking and session replay is past its 30-day window, so 'never clicked' and 'clicked, no response' are indistinguishable in the current data. The fix instruments the whole path so this ambiguity does not recur. Nothing has been committed, pushed, or deployed.

items.text: Optimization page now emits optimized_viewed, export_cta_seen, export_pdf_tapped; download route emits authenticated export_started then exactly one of export_success/export_failed, all tagged with optimization_id, platform=web, source, format, renderer/error code

Regression test failed 3/3 before the fix, passes 3/3 after; targeted + full ESLint clean (11 pre-existing warnings, 0 new errors); production build succeeds; TypeScript clean outside documented pre-existing test errors; smoke test generated a 7,328-byte PDF

iOS (founder-excluded, updated 07-10) now shows 9 resume_uploaded → 2 optimization_completed → 1 export_success — first confirmed real export, corrects the earlier 'zero exports ever' read which was iOS-stale. Web side stays unmeasurable until this fix ships and 5 real completers pass through it
5 uncommitted files on branch codex/fix-web-export-observability, 0 commits ahead of origin/main, nothing deployed or published

tracks.name: Michal / AI Audit Toolkit
tracks.standing: Parked by founder decision 2026-07-09. Michal is migrating from Gemini to OpenAI. We wait until she finishes that move, then set her up on Codex and build 'Michal's OS' — a Codex-based operating layer — instead of the drafted Gemini-Gems path. The audit framing carries over; the Gemini delivery vehicle does not.

items.text: Gemini Track-C prep drafted (4 Gems, voice doc, privacy rules) — framing reusable, delivery vehicle superseded
Blocked on Michal's Gemini → OpenAI migration. No action from us until she's moved.
When she's on OpenAI: set her up on Codex and build Michal's OS; then file the first toolkit template so the pilot feeds the AI Audit Toolkit product.

suggestedSessions.model: GPT-5.6 Sol / Codex - tool-heavy verification across analytics and repository evidence
suggestedSessions.title: Ship web export observability, then measure the real export wall
suggestedSessions.why: One real iOS export is now confirmed, so the old zero-export claim is superseded. Web remains unmeasurable until the prepared observability fix ships and enough real completers pass through it.
suggestedSessions.prompt: In the ResumeBuilder web repo, review and ship the prepared export-observability fix on codex/fix-web-export-observability. Verify its tests and production build, commit and push it, open the appropriate PR, then re-measure the founder-excluded web funnel after 5 real completers: optimized_viewed → export_cta_seen → export_pdf_tapped → export_started → export_success/export_failed. Do not claim the web button is broken without event or reproduction evidence.

suggestedSessions.model: Sonnet tier - well-specified feature against an existing pattern (per GLOBAL-TOOL-USAGE model routing)
suggestedSessions.title: First-run commitment + reminder: turn generated plans into first runs
suggestedSessions.why: The wall is plan-to-run: of 19 installs, 1 generated a plan, 0 ever ran. WP-40 (in flight in another session) fixes Apple Health discoverability; this attacks the same wall from the other side with the already-scored experiment rs-onboarding-001 (WP-20, queued). Do not start until WP-40 merges, both touch the post-onboarding flow.
suggestedSessions.prompt: Implement WP-20 (experiment rs-onboarding-001) in the RunSmart repo: after a training plan is generated, ask the user for a concrete first-run commitment (day + time, one tap options) and schedule a local notification reminder for it. Read the WP-20 packet and docs/agent-os/project-context.md first; follow the existing onboarding/notification patterns; instrument first_run_committed and reminder_fired PostHog events so the plan-to-run funnel can attribute movement. Coordinate with WP-40 (HealthKit activation, branch claude/wp40-healthkit-activation): branch from main only after WP-40 merges to avoid conflicting onboarding changes. Tests included; one story at a time.

growth.note: The latest growth review is logged for 2026-07-07. WP-31 (Hebrew ASO) and WP-32 (Facebook groups) are live inside their measurement windows but still need explicit experiment-log entries so their outcomes can be reviewed cleanly.

traffic.label: Resumely — people opening the app per week (founder excluded)
traffic.story: The launch push worked (26 people, most tried a fit check), then traffic died. This is the number WP-31/32 have to move.

traffic.label: RunSmart — installs per week
traffic.story: Steadier than Resumely, but 0% of installers ever complete a run — volume isn't the problem here, the plan→run step is.

funnels.name: Resumely
steps: Export success (1 real — iOS, as of 07-10)
funnels.story: Updated 07-10: founder-excluded iOS now shows 9 uploaded → 2 optimized → 1 real export success — the first confirmed real export, correcting the prior 'zero exports ever' read (that was stale for iOS). Web export telemetry is still unmeasurable: the old web button had zero click tracking and 7 real users who reached optimization pages left no export signal either way. Fix is written (branch codex/fix-web-export-observability, uncommitted) — full readout: ResumeBuilder IOS APP repo, docs/qa/reports/supabase-post-live-current-state-2026-07-06.md.

rows.resumely: export — 9 uploaded, 2 optimized, 1 real export (iOS, 07-10); web still unmeasurable pre-fix
rows.runsmart: plan→run — 1 of 19 got a plan, 0 ran

activationHeadline.note: No monetization / paid ads until these move (EXD-013). Both funnels founder/QA/bot-excluded per the exclusion rule. Analytics identity hardening shipped 07-07 (PostHog $identify/$create_alias wiring) so future reads can exclude the tester account that owned 22/23 of all Supabase activity. PR #90 (Stories 2-4 upload-picker friction fixes) merged 07-08 — next funnel read should show the effect on real (non-founder) traffic. 07-10: iOS shows a first real export_success (1), and a web observability fix is written (uncommitted, codex/fix-web-export-observability) that closes the zero-click-tracking gap on the web button — 'export is broken' was never actually confirmed for web, only unmeasured.

clocks.what: Activation re-read, both apps (7 immature users each mature by then)
EXD-017 reminder — did you schedule/hold the RunSmart accountant call? Capture recommendation in WP-35
EXD-015 review — did Resumely hit 20% real activation in 30 days?
WP-32 FB groups — 7-day measurement window (posted 07-05, day 3/7)
Michal — set up on Codex, build Michal's OS (parked pending her Gemini→OpenAI migration, decided 2026-07-09)

kk: Both apps passed App Store review and are live (confirmed 07-07): RunSmart 1.0.7(21), Resumely 1.3(8).
Both 0% real D7 activation on mature App Store cohorts (Readout #2).

Resumely wall = export, revised 07-10: iOS now shows 1 real export_success (9 uploaded, 2 optimized) — the 'zero exports ever' claim was iOS-stale, not a live fact. Web export had zero click tracking until today, so 7 real users reaching optimization pages with no export event is not proof of a broken button, only proof of no visibility. Observability fix written, tested (3/3), not yet committed/pushed/deployed.

RunSmart wall = plan→run (onboarding drop 94.7%; 0 runs). WP-40 S1 (Apple Health onboarding step) is implemented and simulator-validated as of 07-10, but uncommitted with no upstream — S2-S4 and physical-device QA still open.
Traffic collapsed post-launch-push; WP-29 web funnel fixed + verified.
One backend tester account owned 22/23 of all Resumely Supabase optimizations pre-hardening — analytics identity/alias wiring shipped 07-07 (Story 1) to fix this going forward.

Garmin: brand attribution + Internal-Test-vs-Commercial tiering are well-grounded in Garmin's public docs (matches 3 real rejections). Fee structure for RunSmart specifically and the Gate 1-4 checklist are founder-confirmed directly with Marc via email/attachments (2026-07-08) — the 07-06 research pass's "unconfirmed" flag was based on Garmin's generic public docs, not RunSmart's actual terms with Marc; no further written confirmation needed.
ku: Do the 7 immature installs/app convert by the 07-12 re-read?
Does FB-group / Hebrew-first posting actually move traffic?
Did Resumely Stories 2-4 (PR #90, merged 07-08) actually move the upload-picker friction numbers for real (non-founder) traffic? Re-read once enough post-merge volume exists.

Does the web export-observability fix (codex/fix-web-export-observability, uncommitted) reveal real web export failures once shipped, or does it turn out the button worked all along and was just untracked? Re-measure by person_id after 5 real web completers, founder/QA-excluded.

Why does no runner start after a plan? (WP-15 fix shipped in 1.0.7(21) — re-read 07-08+.) WP-40 S1 (Apple Health onboarding step) is the other lever on this wall — simulator-validated 07-10, needs S2-S4 + physical-device QA + a real push before it can move this number.
Voice-coach flag: DECIDED 2026-07-09 — deferred (stays off), review at the 07-12 activation re-read or after WP-40 lands. No longer an open question.
uk: Michal Track C fully drafted — blocked only on her live Gemini account.

WP files lag Codex/Cursor sessions — dashboards lie until packets are updated. Three Codex/Cursor sessions found work sitting uncommitted or on detached HEAD this week (ResumeBuilder iOS APP x2, RunSmart Web) 07-06/07/08 — branched, verified, and preserved each time (PR #89, #90, #116), but a recurring risk worth remembering before trusting 'no local changes.' WP-32/33/34 were the exception this round — Cursor updated and pushed the packets itself (commit fe9ad95).

status.json's needsDecision still lists "RunSmart iOS: if build 8 is rejected again" — stale, from before 1.0.7(21) shipped and passed review. Not a portfolio-hq bug; RunSmart iOS's own task files need a refresh to clear it. Same staleness pattern flagged by ./agentic-os verify for COO-LATEST-REVIEW.md (references build 18, current is build 21).
ResumeBuilder IOS APP is missing tasks/ERRORS.md and docs/agent-os/project-context.md — flagged as a required-read gap by two separate Codex sessions now.
RunSmart iOS has ~30 untracked Finder-duplicate '* 2.swift'/'* 2.md' files cluttering the repo — pre-existing, same issue already named in status.json's blockers as blocking clean archives.
uu: Does the PostHog leak run both ways (Resumely → RunSmart project)?
Do other dashboards carry founder/QA contamination (the 50% inflation pattern)?
Is 'activation' the right north star before there's traffic to activate?

weekPriorities.title: Resumely — export-observability fix is ready, ship it before the next re-read
weekPriorities.body: 07-10: iOS's 'zero exports ever' turned out to be stale (1 real export_success now confirmed), and web export was never actually confirmed broken — it just had zero click tracking. A tested fix (3/3 pass, lint clean, build succeeds) instruments the full web export path and is sitting uncommitted on codex/fix-web-export-observability. Commit, push, deploy, then re-measure by person_id after 5 real web completers. WP-31 Hebrew ASO and WP-32 FB-group posts are both live in their measurement windows — traffic is still the binding constraint underneath this.

weekPriorities.title: RunSmart — WP-40 S1 done in simulator, push it before continuing; Garmin stays parked
weekPriorities.body: WP-40 S1 (Apple Health onboarding step) is implemented and simulator-validated as of 07-10 but uncommitted with no upstream — commit and push before starting S2-S4 or physical-device QA. Also re-run the PostHog funnel for the WP-15 first-run fix (1.0.7(21)+) once there's a build-21-only cohort. Garmin stays maintenance-only (EXD-015) — fees and the Gate 1-4 checklist are founder-confirmed directly with Marc (email/attachments), no further confirmation needed; WP-24/26/27/28 stay paused on priority, not on missing information.

weekPriorities.title: Michal — schedule the live Gemini session
weekPriorities.body: Track C fully drafted. One calendar invite unblocks the first paid pilot of the OS.

weekPriorities.title: OS hygiene — keep the record true
weekPriorities.body: End every Codex/Cursor session with 'update the work packet' and 'create a branch before committing' — two Codex sessions this week were found sitting on detached HEAD. Clear the stranded-work board (see Today tab count) and the ResumeBuilder iOS APP missing-file gap (tasks/ERRORS.md, docs/agent-os/project-context.md).

readouts.wall: export: 1 confirmed iOS export; web unmeasurable until observability ships
